title: Platform tenant contract
summary: Every k8s-native deliverable on the nanohype stack ships as a Platform tenant — a self-contained unit the eks-agent-platform operator can scaffold, suspend (via kill-switch), and tear down via CR reconciliation. This contract names the artifacts a chart must produce, the minimum Platform CR shape, the OTel resource attributes the operator expects, and the things you must NOT do inside a chart.

required_artifacts.description: Base values shared across all environments.
Per-env delta files. Required even if empty — tooling consistency depends on the presence of all three.
The workload itself. Stateful workloads use StatefulSet; everything else uses Deployment.
ClusterIP Service exposing the workload to in-cluster consumers.
ServiceAccount used by the pod. `eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn` annotation is rendered from `aws.platformRoleArn` per-env value, pointing at the landing-zone-owned IRSA role.
Default-deny NetworkPolicy plus explicit egress allow-list.
ApplicationSet entry to be registered with `nanohype/eks-gitops` or `nanohype/aks-gitops`.

Platform CR (and any required BudgetPolicy CR) declaring the tenant boundary. The eks-agent-platform operator reconciles Namespace, ResourceQuota, LimitRange, default-deny NetworkPolicy, ArgoCD AppProject, and the per-Platform IRSA role from this CR.
Optional AgentFleet CR composing a kagent Agent + ModelConfig + KEDA scaler. Required only when the app runs an AI agent fleet inside its tenant.

spec.persona: <one of: sales-ops | support | finance | ops | founder | eng | marketing | legal | generic>

otel_resource_attrs.name: agents.platform
otel_resource_attrs.description: The Platform CR name. Used by dashboards to slice by app.

otel_resource_attrs.name: agents.model_family
otel_resource_attrs.description: AI workloads only. One of: anthropic, amazon, meta, mistral, etc. Drives cost attribution and guardrail routing.

otel_resource_attrs.name: agents.model_id
otel_resource_attrs.description: AI workloads only. The full Bedrock model ID being invoked.

do_not: Do NOT scaffold IAM roles inside the chart. The IRSA role is provisioned by the corresponding landing-zone <app>-platform component; the chart's ServiceAccount annotates with its ARN via `aws.platformRoleArn`.
Do NOT add cloud-substrate tofu (VPCs, base IAM, KMS keys) inside the app. Substrate lives in `nanohype/landing-zone` as a per-app `<app>-platform` component.
Do NOT add cluster-level addons in the chart (ingress controller, cert-manager, External Secrets, observability stack). Those are gitops-repo concerns in `nanohype/eks-gitops` or `aks-gitops`.
Do NOT skip per-env `values-{dev,staging,production}.yaml` — every chart has three deltas even if some are empty.
Do NOT hardcode AWS account IDs, region names, or KMS key ARNs in chart values. Per-env values plumb them in from landing-zone outputs at deploy time.
